DISCOVERY 610, APPEARANCES: ROBERT V. CHIN FOR PLAINTIFF AND MOVING PARTY: ALAN T. SEIKI NEAL TRAVESTEIN FOR DEFENDANT AND OPPOSING PARTY: AMERICAN ISUZU MOTORS, INC. AND GENERAL MOTORS CORPORATION HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO RE-OPEN DISCOVERY AND TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO FORM AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED IN PART. THE COURT FINDS GOOD CAUSE TO PERMIT ADDITIONAL DISCOVERY BEYOND THE TERMS OF THE AUGUST 30, 2004 STIPULATION AS TO GENERAL MOTORS CORPORATION (GMC) ONLY. THE DEFENDANT FILED ITS ANSWER TO THE COMPLAINT ON NOVEMBER 22, 2004. DISCOVERY AS TO GMC SHALL BE COMPLETED 30 DAYS BEFORE THE CURRENT TRIAL DATE OF MAY 16, 2005. DEFENDANT GMC IS ORDERED TO PROVIDE SUBSTANTIVE RESPONSES TO THE WRITTEN DISCOVERY PROPOUNDED BY PLAINTIFF ON OR BEFORE FEBRUARY 24, 2005. THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMPLIANCE DATE MODIFIED TO MAR-01-2005. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
